current_baseline.latest_accepted_milestone: Exercise Catalog Utilization / Specialized Movement Coverage v1

active_roadmap.current_authorized_milestone: Test-First Quality Gate Development Plan v1
active_roadmap.recommended_next_milestone_after_accepta: Exercise Eligibility Matrix v1, Catalog Reachability Audit v2, Workout Preview Rolling Exposure Rotation v2, or provider/process planning milestone as needed
active_roadmap.delivery_flow: downloadable patch -> preflight -> apply patch -> validate -> stage explicit files -> commit -> push -> feature snapshot -> Architecture handoff
active_roadmap.process_doctrine: Bite by bite, just bigger bites. Bigger milestone is okay. Bigger single patch is not okay.

complex_backend_quality_gate: Identify the exact failing, missing, or underperforming user path.
Add a failing regression test, diagnostic test, or coverage test where practical.
Confirm the test fails or exposes the gap before implementation.
Apply the smallest safe implementation change.
Re-run the original manual/browser smoke path.

risk_model.low: docs/typo/isolated helper: normal patch and focused validation
risk_model.medium: deterministic service or bounded integration: light diagnostic, focused test, narrow patch, regression validation, smoke if user-visible
risk_model.high: workout generation, catalog selection/scoring, state/persistence, nutrition targets, provider output, recommendations: diagnostic first, failing/coverage test, narrow patch, regression validation, original smoke, Linux/browser smoke, project memory, Architecture acceptance

stop_conditions: same bug survives two implementation patches
tests pass but browser smoke fails
Linux smoke fails after Windows green
candidate pools or data shape are unclear
implementation requires broader scope than approved
patch fails to apply because of drift and next step is not obvious
implementation crosses into deferred v2 scope
process_doctrine.bug_to_regression_rule: Every real smoke failure becomes automated regression test, diagnostic/coverage test, documented limitation, or backlog item.
process_doctrine.v1_v2_scope_rule: Architecture defines v1 acceptance and deferred v2 scope before implementation when a feature could expand.
process_doctrine.provider_ai_rule: Provider may propose. Backend validates. User sees only approved output.

historical_required_context.daily_coach_async_provider_runtime_desig: Daily Coach Async Provider Runtime Design v1 / DAILY_COACH_ASYNC_PROVIDER_RUNTIME_DESIGN_V1_ACCEPTED
historical_required_context.daily_coach_async_persistence_design: Daily Coach Async Persistence Design v1 / DAILY_COACH_ASYNC_PERSISTENCE_DESIGN_V1_ACCEPTED
historical_required_context.daily_coach_async_persistence_contracts: Daily Coach Async Persistence Contracts + Schema v1 / feature/daily-coach-async-persistence-contracts-schema-v1
historical_required_context.disallowed_persistence: provider runtime implementation, raw provider output persistence, rejected provider output persistence are not authorized unless explicitly scoped
historical_required_context.daily_coach_async_persistence_service_sh: Daily Coach Async Persistence Service Shell v1 / feature/daily-coach-async-persistence-service-shell-v1 / service/repository shell only
historical_required_context.model_boundaries: qwen3 is not bridge-enabled; qwen3:32b is research / future premium async candidate only
historical_required_context.not_authorized: normal Today provider call; public async narrative display

runtime_split.windows: Primary development workstation at C:\projects\fitness_ai. Use Windows for patch application, validation, explicit staging, commit, push, and feature snapshots.
runtime_split.linux: Runtime/smoke environment at ~/projects/fitness-ai-platform. Pull feature or main branches after push/merge and run Linux validation when runtime-relevant.

model_policy.qwen2_5_3b: Approved local bridge/default experimental model for direct_ollama nutrition explanation provider paths when explicitly enabled.
model_policy.qwen3: Not approved as a bridge/default model. Do not promote without an explicit model-evaluation milestone.
model_policy.qwen3_32b: Research/future premium async candidate only; not approved for normal runtime.
model_policy.model_promotion: No model may be promoted to default without explicit Architecture acceptance, runtime QA, fallback behavior, and project-memory update.
model_policy.fallback: Deterministic fallback remains mandatory for all provider-backed paths.
model_policy.boundary: Provider may propose only inside approved schemas/contracts. Backend validates. User sees only approved output.

daily_coach_async_boundary.current: Daily Coach async/provider work remains gated by accepted designs and developer-only/manual preview boundaries unless a later milestone explicitly authorizes runtime behavior.
daily_coach_async_boundary.normal_today_behavior: Normal Today behavior remains deterministic and must not block on provider generation.
not_authorized: normal Today provider call by default
public async narrative display without approval
provider runtime promotion without explicit milestone approval

workflow_rules.phase_separated_delivery: Use separate phases: preflight/branch, apply, validate, stage, commit, push, snapshot, Linux pull/smoke.
workflow_rules.no_git_add_dot: Do not use git add .; stage explicit files only.
workflow_rules.temporary_apply_artifacts_outside_repo: Temporary patches and apply scripts stay outside the repo.
workflow_rules.run_apply_scripts_from_repo_as: Run temporary apply scripts from the repo root using python ..\<script>.py.
workflow_rules.apply_raw_patches_from_repo_as: Apply raw patches from the repo root using git apply --check ..\<patch>.patch then git apply ..\<patch>.patch.
workflow_rules.no_broad_formatters_for_docs_only: For docs-only milestones, do not run broad formatters that can rewrite unrelated application files.
workflow_rules.long_handoffs_in_code_blocks: Long handoffs must be delivered as one copy/paste-ready code block.

model_provider_policy.qwen2_5_3b: Approved local bridge/default experimental model for direct_ollama nutrition explanation provider paths when explicitly enabled.
model_provider_policy.qwen3: Not approved as a bridge/default model. Do not promote without an explicit model-evaluation milestone.
model_provider_policy.qwen3_32b: Research/future premium async candidate only; not approved for normal runtime.
model_provider_policy.model_promotion: No model may be promoted to default without explicit Architecture acceptance, runtime QA, fallback behavior, and project-memory update.
model_provider_policy.fallback: Deterministic fallback remains mandatory for all provider-backed paths.
model_provider_policy.boundary: Provider may propose only inside approved schemas/contracts. Backend validates. User sees only approved output.
